commit.message:
Clarify skillpm’s position relative to GitHub CLI/Claude plugin specs (#57)

Users asked what emerging GitHub CLI and Claude plugin specs imply for
skillpm. This PR clarifies that those specs are host/runtime extension
mechanisms, while skillpm remains focused on portable Agent Skills
packaging and orchestration.

- **Documentation: plugin-spec positioning**
  - Added a new section to `README.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs (GitHub CLI / Claude Code)`
  - Added a matching section to `docs/index.md`:
    - `How this relates to plugin specs`
  - Both sections explicitly state:
    - plugin specs and Agent Skills are complementary, not replacements
- skillpm’s model is unchanged (`npm` for deps + `skills` for wiring +
`add-mcp` for MCP config)

- **Docs consistency**
- Kept language aligned across repository README and docs homepage so
users get the same answer regardless of entry point.

Example excerpt added:

```md
- Plugin specs define host-specific extension/runtime hooks.
- Agent Skills define portable instruction packages that can be reused across agent systems.

For skillpm, the model stays the same: npm for dependency management, plus `skills` and `add-mcp` for wiring and MCP configuration.
```

<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->



<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> I read about a plugin spec for GitHub cli and Claude code. What does
that mean for out project?


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT TIPS -->
---

✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for
you](https://github.com/sbroenne/skillpm/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot)
— coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for
your repo.

commit.message:
fix: tutorial-verify.fsx uses Release DLL in CI + fail-fast: false

CI builds in Release mode but tutorial-verify.fsx was referencing
../bin/Debug/net8.0/SageTUI.Library.dll, causing FS0078 error.

Fix: add #if RELEASE ... #else ... #endif to handle both configurations.
CI step now passes --define:RELEASE to dotnet fsi.

Also add fail-fast: false to the matrix strategy so all three OS jobs
complete independently — getting full diagnostic information instead of
cancelling macOS/Windows when Ubuntu fails first.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(sanitize): use testkit assertions in sanitize_test.go (#181)

Replace 50 manual if/t.Errorf assertions with testkit helpers:
- AssertEqual for value comparisons
- AssertTrue/AssertFalse for boolean checks
- AssertNil for nil checks
- Keep t.Fatal guards where pointer dereferences follow

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pdf): atomic re-render with overlay + debounced zoom

Problems fixed:
1. Pages disappeared during zoom — reRenderPages() was removing all page
   elements then rendering one-by-one, causing a blank flash each zoom.
   Fix: render all new pages into an off-screen temp container first, then
   atomically swap (insert before overlay, remove overlay last) so the
   user always sees either the old pages or all new pages — never blank.

2. Rapid scroll events queued many concurrent re-renders — each wheel
   event triggered a full reRenderPages() call immediately.
   Fix: debounce re-render 250ms after the last wheel event. The zoom
   label updates immediately on every tick for responsive feedback; only
   the expensive canvas re-render is deferred.

3. Initial load showed pages one-by-one after removing the spinner.
   Fix: render all pages into temp container while spinner is visible,
   remove spinner and insert all pages atomically in one frame.

CSS:
- via-pdf-scroll gains position:relative for overlay to anchor to
- via-pdf-loading-overlay: position:absolute inset:0 z-index:50 covers
  existing pages during re-render

Bump: 0.1.6 → 0.1.7

Assisted-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
email: implement context cancellation check in Send (#180)

The Send method's doc comment claimed it respects context cancellation
during the dial phase, but the implementation ignored the context
parameter entirely (using _ as the parameter name).

Fix this by:
1. Checking ctx.Err() before initiating the SMTP send
2. Updating the doc comment to accurately describe behavior
3. Using the named ctx parameter instead of _

Added tests:
- TestSend_CancelledContext: verifies cancelled context returns error
- TestSend_DeadlineExceeded: verifies expired deadline returns error

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent concurrent sessions from overwriting other users' threads

Add merge-on-write (saveMemoryMerged) to all hooks that write sticky-note.json.
Before saving, re-reads the file from disk and preserves any threads by ID that
exist on disk but not in memory. This prevents one user's session-end from
silently dropping another user's threads.

Replace fragile merge=union gitattribute with a custom JSON-aware git merge
driver that does a proper 3-way merge of threads by ID. For threads present
in both sides, the version with the latest last_activity_at wins. Threads
unique to either side are preserved.

The merge driver is installed to .sticky-note/merge-driver.js by both init
and update commands, with git configured via merge.sticky-note.driver.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor(media,text): unify module structure

Restructure media and text modules to match zsys standard layout:

  module/
  ├── __init__.py          # Re-exports from bindings
  ├── c/                   # Native C implementation
  │   ├── *.h, *.c
  │   └── CMakeLists.txt
  └── bindings/
      ├── python/          # Python bindings
      │   ├── __init__.py
      │   └── submodules/
      ├── go/
      ├── rust/
      └── ...

This matches telegram/, utils/, and other zsys modules.
Import paths unchanged for end users:
  from zsys.media import image, video
  from zsys.text import html, parse

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

crypto: make random reader injectable for testability (#178)

Refactor GenerateToken and GenerateHexToken to use a package-level
randReader variable (defaulting to crypto/rand.Reader) instead of
calling rand.Read directly. This allows tests to inject a failing
reader to exercise the error paths.

Added tests:
- TestGenerateToken_RandFailure: verifies error wrapping when reader fails
- TestGenerateHexToken_RandFailure: same for hex token generation
- TestGenerateToken_UsesDefaultReader: confirms default is crypto/rand

Coverage: crypto package 95.0% → 100.0%

commit.message:
database: return joined error when both fn and rollback fail in WithTx (#177)

Previously, when a transaction function returned an error and the
subsequent rollback also failed, only the original fn error was returned
to the caller while the rollback error was logged. This made it
impossible for callers to programmatically detect rollback failures.

Now use errors.Join to return both errors, preserving the original
behavior of logging the rollback error while also making it available
to the caller via errors.Is/errors.As.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(health): use testkit assertions in tests (#176)

- health_test.go: AssertEqual, AssertNil, AssertNoError, AssertError, AssertTrue, AssertFalse, AssertNotEqual (20 replacements)
- http_test.go: AssertEqual, AssertNoError, AssertError (14 replacements)
- discovery_test.go: AssertEqual, AssertNoError, AssertError (10 replacements)

Preserves t.Fatalf guards and handler-func assertions.

Co-authored-by: devaloi <166653705+devaloi@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(i18n): translate 12 Dutch strings with correct terminology and ToV (#74)

Fills all 12 empty `msgstr` entries in `app.nl.po`, using terminology
and tone consistent with the existing Dutch catalog.

**Terminology choices derived from existing translations:**

- **"controleren"** not "verifiëren" — matches `"Controleer of de slug
bestaat op WordPress.org"`
- **"Schakel naar"** not "Overschakelen naar" — concise, informal ToV
matching the `je`-form used throughout
- **"Voorinstelling meervoudsvorm"** — readable alternative to the
28-char compound "Meervoudsvormvoorinstelling"
- **"Glossaryweergave"** — "weergave" for viewer; "glossary" kept
untranslated per convention (`Glossarytermen`, `glossary-opties`)
- **"Editorinterface laden..."** — matches existing `"De editorinterface
kon niet worden geladen"`
- **"Taalcode"** — matches `"Doeltaalcode"`

```po
msgid "Switch to dark mode"
msgstr "Schakel naar donkere modus"

msgid "Verify plugin slug"
msgstr "Plugin-slug controleren"

msgid "Plural form preset"
msgstr "Voorinstelling meervoudsvorm"
```

commit.message:
feat(text): add text processing module with C acceleration

- C core library (zsys/text/c/):
  - text.h: API for escape/unescape/strip/truncate/format
  - text.c: UTF-8 aware implementation
  - CMakeLists.txt: Simple build (no external deps)

- Python bindings with C acceleration + pure Python fallback:
  - common/: escape_html, unescape_html, escape_markdown,
             strip_html, strip_markdown, strip_control,
             truncate, split, char_count
  - html/: bold, italic, underline, strikethrough, code, pre,
           spoiler, link, mention, quote
  - markdown/: to_html conversion, formatting helpers
  - parse/: get_args, get_command, extract_urls, extract_mentions,
            extract_hashtags, extract_emails
  - format/: bytes, duration, number, plural (RU/EN), percent, phone

Key features:
- C library for 10-100x speedup on escape/strip operations
- Automatic fallback to pure Python if C lib not available
- Russian plural support: plural(5, ('файл', 'файла', 'файлов'))
- Telegram-specific HTML formatting
- URL/mention/hashtag extraction with regex

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fileWatch CI - resolve symlinks for FSW + increase timing tolerance

Two root causes:
- macOS: /var -> /private/var symlink causes FSEvents to silently deliver
  zero events when watching a path that traverses an unresolved symlink.
  Fix: resolve watchDir via DirectoryInfo.ResolveLinkTarget(true) before
  creating FileSystemWatcher. Dispatch still uses original watchDir + e.Name
  so callers receive the path they provided.
- Ubuntu CI: 'dispatches full absolute path' used 300ms wait; Sub.map (same
  debounce 50ms) uses 400ms and passes. Loaded CI runners can have inotify
  delivery + thread scheduling > 300ms. Increased to 600ms for headroom.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(source-generator): generate async Task<T> wrappers with built-in error unwrapping and strip CgScriptRootFolder prefix from script names

- WrapperEmitter: change generated method return type from Task<ScriptResult<T>> to async Task<T>
  (or async Task for void scripts); call .GetValueOrThrowError() internally so callers receive
  the unwrapped value directly and errors are propagated as exceptions
- CgScriptWrapperGenerator: read CgScriptRootFolder MSBuild property (default 'CgScript') and
  strip that prefix from the script name passed to Execute(), fixing the double 'CgScript/' in
  the path; hint names and namespaces continue to use the full relative path

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(pdf): Ctrl+scroll wheel zoom with focal-point preservation

- Ctrl/Cmd + scroll wheel zooms in/out centred on the pointer position
  (content under cursor stays fixed after re-render)
- Ctrl+0 resets to 100%; Ctrl+= / Ctrl+- step through zoom levels
- Keyboard shortcuts registered via registerDomEvent in onload() so they
  are properly cleaned up when the view is destroyed
- stepZoom() now captures the viewport centre as focal point so button
  clicks also keep the reading position stable
- Zoom button tooltips updated to show keyboard shortcut hints

Bump: 0.1.4 → 0.1.5

Assisted-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add research workflow tools (#69)

Add research workflow tools (generate_research_checklist, update_research_item, get_research) to planning-tools.ts. Includes sourceUrl persistence, XSS sanitization, URL validation, state machine transitions, and 14 integration tests. Fixes #66.

* feat: add GET /api/goals/:id/research endpoint (#70)

Adds research API endpoint with goal ownership validation, seed endpoint with proper error handling, integration tests, and backend docs update. Fixes #67.

- Extended handleToolComplete to render categorized research checklist card\n- Added dark-theme CSS for .research-card matching goal card pattern\n- Added 3 E2E tests: card rendering, SSE wiring, XSS prevention\n- Updated docs/frontend.md SSE event handling table\n- Hoisted VALID_STATUSES to module scope\n- Derived categoryOrder from Object.keys(CATEGORY_LABELS)\n\nCloses #68

feat(skills): add content comparison and nextAction to edit-reply

Add content comparison before editing in handleEditReply to prevent
unnecessary API calls when the new content is identical to the current
message content. Also add nextAction field to the success response to
instruct the agent to exit immediately after a successful edit.

Changes:
- In handleEditReply, fetch current message via fetchMessage before
  calling editMessage; if content is unchanged, return failure early
- fetchMessage failures (null or exception) do not block editing;
  comparison is skipped gracefully
- Comparison is performed after stripXmlTags and unescapeNewlines to
  match the final formatted content
- Add nextAction to the success response data
- Update skills/edit-reply/SKILL.md with documentation for content
  comparison behavior and response examples
- Add 6 new unit tests covering all new behaviors

Resolves #274

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Jim Chen <Jim@ChenJ.im>

commit.message:
feat(media): add native C/FFmpeg media processing module

- C core library (zsys/media/c/):
  - media.h: Full API with error codes, types, function declarations
  - common.c: FFmpeg init, detect_type, get_info
  - image.c: media_image_resize with libswscale
  - audio.c: media_audio_to_voice (OGG Opus), media_audio_extract
  - video.c: media_video_thumbnail, media_video_to_note (square H.264)
  - gif.c: media_gif_from_video, media_gif_to_mp4
  - CMakeLists.txt: Build system linking FFmpeg libs

- Python bindings:
  - common/: ctypes bindings, MediaType/MediaInfo/MediaError
  - image/: resize(), crop() with sync/async variants
  - video/: duration(), thumbnail(), to_note(), transcode(), resize()
  - audio/: to_voice(), extract(), normalize()
  - gif/: from_video(), to_mp4(), optimize()

All operations use in-process FFmpeg (no subprocess) for maximum
performance. Async functions use run_in_executor for non-blocking IO.

Telegram-specific formats:
- Voice: OGG Opus @ 48kHz mono, ~64kbps
- Video note: Square H.264 @ 384px, max 60s
- Animation: H.264 MP4 (converted from GIF)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fileWatch macOS FSEvents symlink resolution + timing

- Use Path.Combine(watchDir, e.Name) instead of e.FullPath
  macOS FSEvents resolves /var -> /private/var in FullPath;
  reconstructing from watchDir preserves the caller-supplied path
- Increase FSW startup sleep 100ms -> 250ms (macOS FSEvents warm-up)
- Increase wait timeouts: debounce test 400->700ms, dir test 400->700ms
- Add 250ms startup sleep in cancellation and Sub.map inline tests
- Sub.map test wait 300->400ms for FSEvents event delivery latency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(pipeline-core): preserve existing workspace fields on update

Use spread merge ({ ...existing, ...update }) instead of direct
assignment when updating a workspace entry, preventing loss of
fields not included in the update payload.

feat(coc): dynamic collapsed sidebar width based on repo name length

Calculate collapsed mini-sidebar width dynamically (~7.5px/char) instead
of using fixed w-44/min-w-[160px] Tailwind classes, so text fits properly
regardless of repo name length.

Also deduplicate normalizeRemoteUrl by importing from pipeline-core
instead of maintaining a local copy in repoGrouping.ts.

commit.message:
docs: add domain research with full brainstorm + availability

Multi-model brainstorm (Claude Opus 4.6 + GPT-5.1) + ~120 DNS checks.

Key insight: tagline already encodes the domain —
  Active Discovery Vision AI for Youth = ADVAY

Top picks:
  advayplays.com  — primary app URL
  helloadvay.com  — marketing/landing site
  advay.world     — brand jewel

~40 available .com domains documented.
Full agent output from both models preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add /refine command (#18)

* Add /refine command (#3)

Middle step between /intake and /knock-out-todos. Prioritizes the
highest-priority open TODO items, adds technical detail and effort
estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL/Unknown) as inline annotations, asks clarifying
questions interactively (supervised) or via GH issue comments (headless),
and commits + opens a PR with proposed updates.

Also adds refine.md to the generate-dist.sh loop, to the respec managed
files table, and documents it in spec.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update .claude/commands/refine.md

commit.message:
fix: discard stale poll results after network switch

Added a generation counter (AtomicU64) that increments on every
network switch. Spawned poll tasks capture the current generation
and check it before sending results — if the generation changed
(network switched while in-flight), results are silently dropped
to prevent cross-network data contamination.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Conditionally hide Area column for repos without area labels

Omit the Area column header and cells entirely when no PR in the
report has any area-* labels. Repos like msbuild that don't use
area- labels get a cleaner table without an empty column.

feat: Sprint 72 P5 Sub.fileWatch GREEN (7 tests)

- FileSystemWatcher-based Sub with debounce for file and directory paths
- Watches parent dir+filename filter for files, dir+*.* for directories
- Single Timer instance reset on each event (no per-event allocation)
- Volatile.Read/Write for pendingPath shared between FSW and Timer threads
- Handlers registered BEFORE EnableRaisingEvents to avoid Carmack race
- [<EditorBrowsable(Never)>] on DragSub/PasteSub to hide internal cases
- Fix test timing: runSubWithActions helper ensures FSW starts before file writes
- README: 2,784 → 2,797 tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
